strategies.hypothesis: A single independent answer is the minimum-cost baseline.
strategies.name: Direct One

strategies.hypothesis: A small independent plurality is a stable low-cost baseline.
strategies.name: Five-Vote Plurality

strategies.hypothesis: Expanding the shared independent bank from five to nine may improve exact-answer coverage and stabilize candidate support, while retaining the only review mechanism with a positive observed correction balance.
strategies.name: Nine Solvers plus Three Falsifying Repairers

strategies.hypothesis: If missing-answer coverage is the main remaining bottleneck, expanding the independent bank from nine to fifteen while preserving the validated falsifying-repair mechanism should raise weakest-family and pooled exact accuracy without changing the orchestration principle.
strategies.name: Fifteen Solvers plus Three Falsifying Repairers

strategies.hypothesis: Because most successful repairs did not create a new correct answer, three frequency-blind comparative reviewers may more reliably recover an existing minority candidate while the strict-majority trigger protects unusually coherent base outcomes.
strategies.name: Guarded Nine-Bank Candidate Adjudication

strategies.hypothesis: Independent rederivation may reduce anchoring to fragmented candidates, while delegate fusion gives the base plurality one protective vote without reintroducing the full correlated base pool.
strategies.name: Guarded Rederivation with Delegate Fusion
